response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a long career in web and full-stack development with a primary stack centered on JavaScript, TypeScript, and Node.js. While he has tangential GIS/geospatial experience through the GISCollective and Green Map projects, and some historical C++ work at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a thesis project, his commercial experience does not include modern C++ or Rust development, routing data pipelines, or geospatial tile processing at production scale. His career trajectory has moved further from systems programming over time, with his current and recent roles at Stripe and Shopify being firmly in the web/TypeScript domain. The core technical requirements of this role — modern C++ (or Rust), routing tile pipelines, and navigation system engineering — are not represented in any substantive way.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS domain exposure through GISCollective, Green Map, and a FOSS4G conference talk
Distributed systems and high-throughput services experience (millions of HTTP req/s at Dunnhumby)
Breadth of experience across embedded, desktop, web, and mobile gives general systems intuition
concerns: No modern or commercial-grade C++ experience; last C++ work was 2014–2015 and a university thesis
No Rust experience listed anywhere in the resume
Zero routing, navigation, or map tile pipeline experience — the core domain of the role
